places.name: Fischerstube
places.near: Tram to Rheingasse/Marktplatz — walk to Rheingasse 45
places.order: Cordon bleu / Wiener schnitzel / calf's liver / Ueli draft beer
places.notes: مطعم صيد الأسماك
places.famousFor: Home of Ueli Bier craft brewery since 1974 / Kleinbasel
places.goodToKnow: Walk-in friendly / $$-$$$ / beer garden + cellar

places.name: Hotel Bischofhof - Bistro Jasmin
places.near: No metro/tram — taxi/drive / Griesbruck, Isarco Valley
places.order: Tasting menu on request / homemade pasta / local meats
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Former Michelin-level kitchen / bistro fare / 650-label wine list
places.goodToKnow: Book ahead / $$$
places.address: Griesbruck, 4, 39043 Klausen, Autonome Provinz Bozen - Südtirol, Italy

places.name: Il Tinello
places.order: House-made pasta; steaks
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Welcoming osteria in old Bolzano with house-made pasta
places.goodToKnow: Via dei Conciapelli; closed Sundays; average prices, good service

places.name: In Viaggio - Claudio Melis
places.near: The restaurant relocated from Bolzano to Merano inside Hotel Villa Irma in Maia Alta; roughly a 30-40 min drive from Bolzano. Evening service only, reservation essential
places.order: The 5-, 7- or 9-course tasting menu (approx. 138/168/188 euro); dishes blending Alpine and global ingredients
places.notes: 1 MICHELIN Star. Now located in Merano, not Bolzano city, though historically the Bolzano reference. (Needs verification of exact current opening days)
places.famousFor: Sardinian-born chef Claudio Melis's intimate 1-star 'culinary voyage'; only a handful of tables with the chef explaining each course
places.goodToKnow: Dinner only, ~4 days a week (closed Sun/Mon/Tue); tiny dining room so book well ahead. Chef's wife Monica Wieser runs front of house
places.address: Via Belvedere 17, 39012 Merano (Maia Alta), South Tyrol

places.name: Zur Kaiserkron
places.cuisine: Contemporary Mediterranean with South Tyrolean touches
places.near: Central old town on Piazza della Mostra, a short walk from Piazza Walther; easy on foot
places.order: Seasonal tasting menu; signature plates such as fig-and-veal-sweetbread risotto, roast venison; ask about the gourmet vs. casual offering
places.notes: Listed in the MICHELIN Guide; highly rated (~4.7) and popular for couples/luxury dining.
places.famousFor: Elegant vaulted dining rooms in historic Palazzo Pock; refined seasonal degustation cuisine by chef Filippo Sinisgalli; one of Bolzano's top special-occasion tables
places.goodToKnow: Two concepts under one roof (Casual + Gourmet); extensive local/Italian wine list. Closed Sun and Mon; lunch and dinner Tue-Sat. Reservations recommended

places.name: Restaurant Laurin (Parkhotel Laurin)
places.cuisine: Modern Italian with local specialities; meat and fish
places.near: In the Parkhotel Laurin, Via Laurin, a few minutes' walk from the station and Piazza Walther
places.order: Seasonal Italian dishes with modern technique; the small section of traditional South Tyrolean specialities; dining in the garden in summer
places.notes: Listed in the MICHELIN Guide; among the best-regarded upscale restaurants in the city.
places.famousFor: Liberty/Art Nouveau dining room and a romantic tree-shaded garden terrace at the historic Parkhotel Laurin (since 1910); one of Bolzano's most elegant settings
places.goodToKnow: Beautiful frescoed interior and famous garden; a strong choice for a romantic or luxury dinner. Reservations advised

places.name: Löwengrube
places.cuisine: Traditional Alpine + classic Italian with a contemporary twist
places.near: Central old town, near Piazza della Dogana; easy on foot
places.order: Carbonara tortelloni with parmesan cream and crispy bacon; seasonal Italian plates; a bottle chosen from the historic cellar
places.notes: Listed in the MICHELIN Guide (not starred).
places.famousFor: One of Bolzano's oldest houses (a restaurant since the 16th century) with a wine cellar dating to 1280 and ~1,000 labels; refined modern Italian cooking
places.setting: Upscale restaurant with historic wine cellar
places.goodToKnow: Atmospheric historic vaulted rooms; strong for wine lovers though it is a full restaurant (not a drinks-only wine bar). Open Mon-Sat, closed Sun

places.name: Wirtshaus Vögele
places.near: Via Goethe 3, in the heart of the old town just off Piazza Erbe; on foot
places.order: Vögele's Knödel-Kistel (dumpling sampler), homemade Schlutzkrapfen with spinach-cheese filling, speck, and the best Kaiserschmarren in town
places.notes: MICHELIN Bib Gourmand 2026 (awarded 2024). Great for both families and traditional-cuisine seekers.
places.famousFor: Beloved historic inn in a Biedermeier-style building, home to 'the oldest regulars' table in South Tyrol'; a must-visit institution for traditional dishes
places.goodToKnow: Spread over several floors with cozy wood-panelled stube rooms; family- and visitor-friendly, open from morning coffee to dinner. Closed Sun; reservations recommended

places.name: Casa al Torchio (Torgglhaus)
places.area: Bolzano old town (near Piazza delle Erbe/Obstplatz)
places.near: Via Museo 2/A, by the fruit market (Obstplatz) in the old town; on foot
places.order: Canederli (dumplings), Spätzle, Schlutzkrapfen; traditional plates with a light creative touch
places.notes: Long-standing, well-reviewed traditional address in the historic centre.
places.famousFor: Considered the oldest restaurant in Bolzano (an inn since 1786); characterful vaulted rooms across multiple floors
places.goodToKnow: ~90 seats over two floors in three atmospheric rooms; family-friendly and central. Also does pizza

places.name: Franziskanerstuben
places.area: Bolzano old town (near Piazza delle Erbe)
places.cuisine: South Tyrolean + Italian, daily-changing seasonal menu
places.near: Near Piazza delle Erbe/Franziskanergasse in the old town; on foot
places.order: Whatever is on the daily seasonal menu; classic Tyrolean dumplings and meats; regional wines
places.notes: Well-reviewed (~4.3, 2,300+ reviews). (Needs verification of exact house number) (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Warm, beautifully decorated stube atmosphere in the historic centre; traditional Tyrolean dishes with a modern twist using regional produce
places.goodToKnow: Cozy and intimate; good for couples and traditional-cuisine lovers. Reservations recommended

places.name: Hopfen & Co. (Bozner Bier)
places.near: Piazza delle Erbe (Obstplatz) 17, in the heart of the old town; on foot
places.order: Pork knuckle (Stelze), grilled meats and sausages, dumplings; house-brewed beer to accompany (food is the focus, not a drinks-only venue)
places.notes: Popular traditional tavern; food-led despite the on-site brewery. (Needs verification of current website URL)
places.famousFor: Atmospheric house brewery set in a former corner tower of the medieval city walls (with an old prison); lively, museum-like setting on the produce market square
places.goodToKnow: Buzzy and family-friendly; can be busy at peak times. A characterful, Instagrammable historic interior

places.name: Batzen Häusl (Batzenhäusl)
places.near: Via Andreas Hofer 30, edge of the old town; on foot
places.order: Canederli (dumplings), schnitzel, grilled meats, meat-and-cheese platters; house beer alongside
places.notes: Food-focused despite the brewery; well-reviewed local favourite. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Historic house given a hip, modern-stube treatment; locally brewed beer paired with regional dishes
places.setting: Traditional Stube / gastropub with house brewery
places.goodToKnow: Relaxed, contemporary atmosphere that still feels traditional; good for families and casual dinners

places.name: Nadamas
places.cuisine: Italian and Mediterranean, tapas-style plates + local dishes
places.near: Piazza delle Erbe, in the old town; on foot
places.order: Tapas-style sharing plates, speck and cheese boards, pasta; South Tyrolean-Italian crossovers
places.notes: Well-reviewed old-town standby. (Needs verification of exact address and website)
places.famousFor: Lively, popular spot right on the produce market with good-value creative cooking; a favourite for a relaxed meal
places.goodToKnow: Friendly service and good value; can get busy. Good for couples and casual groups

places.name: Fischbänke (Fischbänke Bozen)
places.near: Via Dr. Josef Streiter (Streitergasse), in the old town; on foot
places.order: Fresh fish and seafood small plates; a glass of local white to accompany (food is the draw)
places.notes: Distinctive local favourite. (Needs verification of exact house number and current hours)
places.famousFor: Beloved, quirky spot set around old marble fish-market benches; a very Instagrammable, atmospheric local institution
places.goodToKnow: Casual and popular; limited seating on the old stone benches; great for a light, scenic bite. Can be busy

places.name: Cavallino Bianco (Weisses Rössl)
places.near: Via dei Bottai (Bindergasse), central old town; on foot
places.order: Knödel (dumplings), Schlutzkrapfen, Kaiserschmarren; hearty roast and cured meats
places.notes: Established local tavern. (Needs verification of exact house number and current opening hours)
places.famousFor: Long-standing, rustic Bolzano institution serving classic Tyrolean fare in a cozy tavern setting
places.goodToKnow: Reasonably priced, welcoming and family-friendly; a reliable traditional choice

places.name: Thaler's Knödelbistro
places.near: Central old town; on foot
places.order: The range of canederli/Knödel (speck, cheese, spinach, and sweet versions)
places.notes: Charming specialist for dumplings. (Needs verification of exact address, website and current hours)
places.famousFor: 'The prettiest canederli in town,' from the historic Bolzano-born Thaler house (founded 1763); a photogenic, focused dumpling stop
places.goodToKnow: Great for a quick, authentic and Instagrammable lunch; casual setting

places.name: Il Corso Pizzeria Ristorante
places.near: In Bolzano; check location when booking
places.order: Their signature soft, golden-crust Neapolitan pizzas; seasonal toppings
places.notes: Well-regarded for quality Neapolitan pizza. (Needs verification of exact address and website)
places.famousFor: Elegant pizzeria run by father-and-son team Santo and Francesco Gabriele; artisanal dough blending 00 and whole-grain flours
places.goodToKnow: A cut above the average pizzeria; good for families and casual dinners

places.name: Matté Pizzeria Napoletana
places.near: In Bolzano; check location when booking
places.order: Classic Margherita and seasonal Neapolitan pizzas with a puffy, well-fermented crust
places.notes: Top-rated local pizzeria. (Needs verification of exact address and website)
places.famousFor: One of the highest-rated pizzerias in Bolzano (rated ~9.4 on TheFork) for authentic Neapolitan pizza
places.goodToKnow: Popular; reservations advisable on weekends. Family-friendly

places.name: Fermento
places.near: In Bolzano; check location when booking
places.order: Signature long-fermentation pizzas; seasonal specials
places.notes: Strong contemporary pizzeria. (Needs verification of exact address and website)
places.famousFor: Very highly rated (~9.4 on TheFork) for slow-fermented, high-quality pizza
places.goodToKnow: Modern setting, popular with locals; book ahead on weekends
places.address: Fermento, 11c, Via Palermo - Palermostraße, Europa-Novacella - Europa-Neustift, Bolzano - Bozen, Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol, 39100, Italia

places.name: Pizzium Bolzano
places.near: About an 8-minute walk from the South Tyrol Museum of Archaeology (Ötzi museum); central
places.order: Regional-themed Neapolitan pizzas; classic Margherita
places.notes: Consistent Neapolitan pizza. (Needs verification of exact street address)
places.famousFor: Well-known authentic Neapolitan pizzeria chain outpost; consistent quality and variety
places.goodToKnow: Reliable, family-friendly, good for a casual meal
places.address: Bolzano city centre (near the Ötzi museum)

places.name: Gasthof Kohlern 1899
places.near: By the historic Kohlern/Colle cable car from near the station (city buses 1/6/9 to the lower station), or by car up Via Colle. Note: the cable car finishes early, so for dinner drive up or stay overnight
places.order: Refined South Tyrolean specialities; regional wines; a coffee/strudel on the terrace with the valley view
places.notes: Highly scenic; verify cable-car timetable before planning an evening visit.
places.famousFor: Breathtaking panoramic terrace high above the Bolzano basin; historic 1899 mountain inn (linked to the world's first passenger cable car); Zirbenstube pine room and Belle Epoque veranda
places.setting: Scenic mountain Gasthaus / restaurant with panoramic terrace
places.goodToKnow: One of the most Instagrammable views over Bolzano; excellent for couples and a special lunch. Reserve, especially for the terrace

places.name: Patscheiderhof
places.cuisine: Light, modern South Tyrolean rooted in tradition
places.near: In Signato on the Ritten, ~15 min drive from Bolzano; alternatively drive the Rittner road ~7 km to Unterinnerhof and walk a ~30-min forest trail
places.order: The dumpling trio (Knödel sampler), homemade Schlutzkrapfen, seasonal game/pasta; house speck
places.notes: Official website is active; some aggregator listings mistakenly flag it closed, so confirm current opening days when booking. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: Award-winning country restaurant on the Ritten with panoramic views over Bolzano; famous walnut-tree sun terrace and beautiful courtyard
places.goodToKnow: Popular scenic destination; a former Buschenschank with a 'Goldene Kastanie' Törggelen award. Book ahead, especially for the terrace

places.name: Signaterhof
places.near: In the hamlet of Signato on the Ritten, a short scenic drive from Bolzano (or via the Ritten cable car + local road)
places.order: Local, refined seasonal dishes; dumplings and Tyrolean classics; dine under the walnut tree on the terrace
places.notes: Long-standing Slow Food-recognised country restaurant with valley views.
places.famousFor: Family-run farmhouse inn with a splendid view of Bolzano; a Slow Food 'Osterie d'Italia' address (awarded the top 'chiocciola'/snail) with 18 accolades
places.setting: Scenic Slow Food Gasthof / traditional restaurant
places.goodToKnow: Historic panelled farmhouse parlour plus a sunny terrace; excellent for couples and food lovers seeking authentic quality. Reserve ahead

places.name: Ristorante - Hotel Ansitz Kematen
places.near: Via Caminata 29, Collalbo on the Ritten plateau; reachable by the Ritten cable car + local transfer or by car (~20-30 min from Bolzano)
places.order: Seasonal gourmet menu built on estate-grown ingredients; regional specialities
places.notes: Well-regarded historic estate restaurant on the Ritten.
places.famousFor: ~750-year-old manor estate on a sunny hill above Klobenstein with mountain views; refined cuisine using produce from its own garden and cellar
places.goodToKnow: Beautiful historic residence with terrace and garden; strong for couples and a refined countryside meal. Hot meals lunch and dinner (Mon dinner only); reserve

places.name: Anita
places.near: In the city centre; on foot
places.order: Seasonal Italian dishes; the cheese tasting and soups are singled out by guests
places.notes: Well-reviewed central Italian option. (Needs verification of exact address and website)
places.famousFor: Popular central restaurant praised for excellent quality-to-price and attentive service
places.goodToKnow: Good for couples and small groups; reservations advisable. Reliable, welcoming service
places.address: Anita, 5a, Piazza delle Erbe - Obstplatz, Centro Storico - Altstadt, Centro-Piani-Rencio - Zentrum-Bozner Boden-Rentsch, Rentsch - Rencio, Bolzano - Bozen, Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol, 39100, Italia

places.name: Vineria Paradeis (Löwengang / Alois Lageder)
places.near: In Magrè/Margreid on the South Tyrolean Wine Road, ~25-30 min drive south of Bolzano
places.order: Seasonal market-driven plates using regional and estate produce (the food is the focus); pair with an estate wine if desired
places.notes: Outside Bolzano city but a standout scenic/foodie destination nearby. (Needs verification of current opening days)
places.famousFor: Beautiful courtyard bistro at the Alois Lageder estate built on a biodynamic, near-natural philosophy; a design-forward, Instagrammable setting
places.goodToKnow: A food-led bistro (not a drinks-only wine bar); lovely for a scenic lunch. Check seasonal opening days and book
places.address: Vicolo dei Conti 9, 39040 Magrè sulla Strada del Vino (Margreid)

places.name: Antholzer See
places.near: In the Antholz/Anterselva valley, South Tyrol, at 1,642 m; paid parking at the Biathlon Center or bus 431
places.order: Easy ~4 km lakeside nature-trail loop, photography of the blue water, summer hiking start point, winter ice-skating when frozen
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Third-largest lake in South Tyrol, a strikingly blue alpine lake in the Rieserferner-Ahrn Nature Park
places.goodToKnow: Best in summer/early autumn; flat well-maintained trail; protected nature-park lake (no motorboats)
places.address: Antholzer See, 39030 Rasen-Antholz, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Pragser Wildsee
places.near: In the Prags valley, South Tyrol; reached by car or seasonal bus from Toblach/Dobbiaco; access is regulated in peak summer
places.order: Renting a wooden rowboat and walking the ~3.5 km loop trail around the shore
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: One of the most photographed lakes in the Dolomites (Lago di Braies), with emerald-turquoise water ringed by sheer peaks and a classic wooden boathouse.
places.goodToKnow: Sunrise is the iconic shot. Summer access is restricted (timed entry / shuttle) to manage crowds; very busy midday.
places.address: Pragser Wildsee, 39030 Prags, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Fischzuchtpark Brixen
places.near: On the edge of Brixen (Bressanone), South Tyrol
places.order: Feed the ducks and watch the trout ponds; easy family stroll
places.notes: بحيرة البط
places.famousFor: Small fish-farm and pond park ('duck lake') in Brixen with stocked ponds and a family-friendly green area
places.goodToKnow: Note: 'بحيرة البط' (duck lake); low-key local spot, some on-site fishing/dining
places.address: 39042 Brixen, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Altes Holzhaus Pragser Wildsee
places.near: Head of the Pragser/Braies valley, South Tyrol; lakeshore loop trail
places.order: Rent a wooden rowboat from the boathouse; the emerald lake reflecting the Dolomites
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: The historic wooden boathouse on Pragser Wildsee (Lake Braies) - the postcard rowboat-and-mountains scene
places.goodToKnow: Summer access to the valley is regulated (timed entry / shuttle); go early to beat crowds
places.address: 39030 Prags, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Lake Braies
places.near: End of Val di Braies, South Tyrol, ~30 min from Brunico/Bruneck
places.order: The wooden rowboats and the easy 3.5km lakeshore loop; sunrise reflections of Croda del Becco
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Pragser Wildsee - the emerald jewel of the Dolomites, among the most photographed lakes in the Alps
places.goodToKnow: Peak-season car access restricted (online slot / shuttle); swimming not permitted, boating in summer

places.name: Olanger Stausee
places.near: Near Olang/Valdaora villages at the foot of Kronplatz, South Tyrol
places.order: Flat, family-friendly loop walk with Dolomite views; calm reflections
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Quiet artificial lake near Olang/Valdaora in the Kronplatz area, ringed by an easy lakeside walking path
places.goodToKnow: Low-key local recreation spot; gentle path suitable for strollers and bikes
places.address: Lago di Valdaora, Provincia autonoma di Bolzano - Alto Adige, Italy

places.name: Karersee
places.near: About 20 km south-east of Bolzano in the municipality of Welschnofen/Nova Levante, on the road up the Eggental/Val d'Ega valley; paid parking and a tunnel underpass to the lakeshore.
places.order: The classic reflection viewpoint where the Latemar range mirrors in the still emerald water; walk the easy loop trail around the shore.
places.notes: بحيرة جميلة
places.famousFor: A small Dolomites alpine lake (1,520 m) in the Latemar forest, nicknamed the 'Rainbow Lake' for its emerald-green colour and mirror reflections of the Latemar peaks.
places.goodToKnow: Swimming and getting in the water are not allowed (protected lake). Reflections are best on a calm, windless morning; the lake is fed by underground springs so levels vary.
places.address: Karersee, 39056 Welschnofen, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Vista Panoramica Tre Cime Lavaredo
places.near: Toll road to Rifugio Auronzo (approx €30/car), then walk the loop trail; or hike up from Misurina.
places.order: The three towers lined up at golden hour; the 4km loop trail past Rifugio Locatelli gives the classic head-on composition.
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Panoramic viewpoint over the Tre Cime di Lavaredo, the three sheer rock pinnacles that are the symbol of the Dolomites (UNESCO).
places.goodToKnow: Best light at sunrise/sunset on the north faces. Toll road and parking fill early; arrive before 8am in summer.
places.address: Lunga Via delle Dolomiti, 39012 Brunico BZ

places.name: Toblacher See
places.near: Roadside on the SS51 just south of Dobbiaco/Toblach; car park at the lake, easy flat lakeside trail.
places.order: Mirror reflections of the surrounding peaks; the wooden boardwalk and reed-lined shore make strong foregrounds.
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Toblacher See (Lago di Dobbiaco), an alpine lake at the mouth of the Höhlensteintal valley framed by forest and Dolomite peaks.
places.goodToKnow: Free; quiet early morning for glassy water. Pretty in autumn when larches turn gold.
places.address: Toblacher See, 39034 Toblach, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Seiser Alm
places.near: Cable car from Ortisei or Compatsch; road access to Compatsch is restricted to certain hours for non-residents.
places.order: Wide sweep of meadow leading to the jagged Sassolungo; isolated mountain huts and grazing scenes at sunrise.
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Europe's largest high-alpine meadow, rolling green pastures and wooden chalets beneath the Sassolungo and Sciliar (Schlern) massifs.
places.goodToKnow: Golden side-light at sunrise/sunset is best. Private cars banned on the plateau 9am-5pm; use the cable car or shuttle.
places.address: Alpe di Siusi, Provincia autonoma di Bolzano - Alto Adige, Italy

places.name: St.-Jakobs-Kirche
places.near: In the hamlet of St. Jakob in Val di Funes (Villnöss); reachable by car, small parking nearby.
places.order: Church framed by green meadows with the Odle wall behind; look for foreground hay barns and wildflowers.
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: St. Jakob (San Giacomo), one of the photogenic Baroque village churches of Val di Funes set against the Odle/Geisler peaks.
places.goodToKnow: Free to view from outside; quietest early. Keep to marked paths as surrounding meadows are private.

places.name: Church of St. John
places.near: Park at Ranui (Zans area) in Val di Funes; short walk to the fenced viewing area.
places.order: The lone chapel centered against the dramatic Odle wall; classic shot from the designated viewpoint platform above the meadow.
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Church of St. Johann in Ranui (San Giovanni), the tiny 1744 Baroque chapel that is one of the most photographed spots in South Tyrol, standing alone beneath the Odle peaks.
places.goodToKnow: Approx €4 paid access to the photo viewpoint; best in morning or at dusk. Drones/tripods often restricted; land is private, stay on paths.

places.name: Stelvio Pass
places.near: SS38 mountain road over the Stelvio Pass in South Tyrol, near the Swiss border; drivable in summer only
places.order: The serpentine of hairpins stacked up the mountainside from an overlook; a car or cyclist mid-bend on the switchbacks
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: One of the highest paved Alpine passes, legendary for its tightly stacked hairpin switchbacks
places.goodToKnow: Free; open roughly June–October, closed by snow in winter; go early to beat traffic and for clear light; the eastern (Prato) side has the iconic 48 hairpins
places.address: Stelvio Pass, 23032 Stilfs, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Passo di Stalle
places.near: Pass road from Antholz/Anterselva valley; narrow Austrian side has timed one-way traffic (light-controlled, hourly windows)
places.order: The winding hairpin road snaking up the green pass, or Lake Obersee just over the border crossing; wide-angle alpine panorama
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Staller Sattel / Passo di Stalle, a 2,052 m alpine pass between South Tyrol and East Tyrol, with hairpin bends and nearby Lake Obersee
places.goodToKnow: Road usually open mid-May to end of October only; come early for the lake reflections and to beat the timed-gate queues
places.address: Passo di Stalle, 39030 Rasen-Antholz, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Fly-Line Wasserfall
places.near: Near Campo Tures/Sand in Taufers; reach the start via a ~40 min hike along the three Reiner waterfalls from Winkl/Kematen.
places.order: 850 m Fly-Line zipline ride over the Riva Waterfalls (~5.5 min)
places.notes: Zip line بس متعب الوصول له
places.famousFor: Scenic zipline/flying fox beside the Riva (Reiner) Waterfalls combining zipline, coaster and toboggan elements over a 200 m descent.
places.goodToKnow: Open Easter to early October; riders 15-120 kg, no age limit. Note: the access hike is fairly strenuous.
places.address: Wasserfallweg, 39032 Sand in Taufers, Autonome Provinz Bozen - Südtirol, Italy

places.name: Adventure Park & Fly Line "Emozion Col de Flam"
places.near: At the Seceda cable car valley station in Ortisei (Val d'Anna); reachable by escalator from the village centre
places.order: The Fly Line and zipline plus the high-ropes courses; kids' course and a small animal park/pony rides too
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: 'Emozion Col de Flam' high-ropes adventure park with a 680m Fly Line and zipline at the foot of the Seceda cable car in Ortisei
places.goodToKnow: Open roughly mid-June to end of September; kids' course suits ages ~4-12; paid parking at the cable car station

places.name: Seceda
places.near: Reached via the Seceda cable cars from Ortisei (St. Ulrich), Val Gardena
places.order: The Seceda ridge viewpoint at the top of the cable car; sunrise/sunset light is spectacular
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Seceda — the dramatic tilted-grass summit ridge and Odle peaks viewpoint, one of the most photographed spots in the Dolomites
places.goodToKnow: Only worthwhile in clear weather; high alpine, so bring warm layers and proper shoes for the ridge walks
places.address: Seceda, 39046 Urtijëi, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Col Raiser
places.near: Lower station near St. Christina (Santa Cristina) in Val Gardena
places.order: Ride the Col Raiser gondola up for easy meadow walks and Odle peak views; mountain hut lunch
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Col Raiser cable car and high alpine meadow plateau above Selva/St. Christina, gateway to Puez-Odle hikes
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal operation (summer/winter); a popular, family-friendly starting point for Dolomites trails
places.address: Via Raiser, 65, 39048 Selva di Val Gardena BZ

places.name: Alpin Bob Meran 2000
places.near: Beside the summit station of the Meran 2000 cable car above Merano; reachable car-free via the cable car
places.order: The Alpin Bob coaster ride, racing up to 12 m above ground through forest and meadows
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Italy's longest alpine coaster, a year-round 1.1 km rail toboggan run high above Merano in the Meran 2000 ski-and-hike area
places.goodToKnow: Runs summer and winter; closed mid-March to end of April and in November; single rides or multi-ride packs

places.name: Klausberg-Flitzer
places.near: Reached by the Klausberg cable car from Cadipietra/Steinhaus (Valle Aurina), South Tyrol; the coaster station is next to the Family Park
places.order: The 1,800 m rail coaster with a 360-degree loop, jumps and banked turns reaching up to 40 km/h; combine with the adjacent Family Park and zipline
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Klausberg-Flitzer, the longest alpine coaster (toboggan run) in Italy at 1,800 m, in the Skiarena Klausberg above Cadipietra/Steinhaus in the Ahrntal valley
places.goodToKnow: Runs in summer and winter (closed spring and late autumn with the cable car); minimum age 3, you control your own speed with the brake lever.
places.address: Gemeinde, 39030 Gemeinde Ahrntal, Autonome Provinz Bozen - Südtirol, Italy

places.name: Husky Sled Dog
places.near: Tours run on the Würzjoch (Passo delle Erbe) and Gardertal area near San Martino in Badia; meeting point provided on booking, reach by car
places.order: A husky sled tour over the Würzjoch/Passo delle Erbe — short 'taster' runs, daytime rides, or atmospheric dusk and night tours on the same sled
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Husky Sled Dog — guided dog-sledding (sleddog) experiences with Siberian huskies through the snow-covered Dolomites and South Tyrol valleys
places.goodToKnow: Winter activity, booking required; children from age 3 can join (carried by the instructor); dress warmly.
places.address: STRADA BOERZ, 26, 39030 San Martino in Badia BZ

places.name: Twenty
places.order: Fashion, books, cosmetics, electronics, sportswear; large supermarket on site
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Bolzano's main shopping mall on Via Galileo Galilei, ~5 million visitors a year
places.goodToKnow: 200+ brands in ~80 shops; 6 cinemas, VR arena, KUNI kids park. Mon-Sat 9-19:30, Sun/holidays 10-19:30

places.name: Alpe Pragas
places.order: Premium fruit spreads/jams (75% fruit, no preservatives), compotes, fruit in syrup, syrups, chutneys
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: South Tyrolean 'fruit manufactory' and panorama shop in Braies, Dolomites (founded 1997)
places.goodToKnow: Braies di Fuori 38. Recognized by Gambero Rosso. Mon-Fri 8-12 & 13-18, Sat 9-12:30 & 13:30-18
places.address: Frazione Braies di Fuori, 38, 39030 Braies BZ

places.name: San Candido
places.near: Innsbruck Airport (INN) or Venice (VCE/TSF); train via Innichen station
places.order: Tre Cime di Lavaredo hikes, Collegiate Church of San Candido (Romanesque), summer cycling along the Drava, winter skiing
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Alpine Dolomites town (German: Innichen) in South Tyrol, gateway to the Three Peaks / Tre Cime di Lavaredo
places.goodToKnow: Summer for hiking, winter for skiing; charming pastel Tyrolean village, German-speaking
places.address: 39038 San Candido, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Vipiteno
places.near: Innsbruck Airport (INN), about 55 km north; on the Brenner route
places.order: Zwölferturm (Tower of Twelve), pastel Neustadt main street, Christmas market, nearby Roßkopf ski area
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Medieval South Tyrol town (German: Sterzing), one of Italy's prettiest villages with its colorful main street and Zwölferturm tower
places.goodToKnow: Year-round; Alpine ski town in winter, hiking base in summer, strong Tyrolean character
places.address: 39049 Vipiteno, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Santa Maddalena Alta
places.near: Bolzano Airport (BZO) ~45 min; Innsbruck (INN) ~1.5 hr as alternative
places.order: The Panoramaweg viewpoint photo of the church against the Odle range; the nearby St. Johann (San Giovanni) church
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Iconic Dolomites hamlet in Val di Funes whose church of Santa Maddalena sits beneath the jagged Odle/Geisler peaks - one of the most photographed scenes in the Alps
places.goodToKnow: Best Jun-Sep (green meadows) and snowy winter; quiet alpine valley, peaks glow at sunset
places.address: 39040 Santa Maddalena Alta, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: Corvara
places.near: Bolzano Airport (BZO), ~1.5 hr drive; Innsbruck (INN) or Venice/Treviso as alternatives
places.order: Sellaronda ski circuit in winter, summer hiking under the Dolomite peaks, and Ladin/South Tyrolean cuisine
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Alta Badia ski and hiking village in the South Tyrol Dolomites, part of the Sellaronda ski circuit beneath the Sella massif
places.goodToKnow: Best Dec-Mar (ski) and Jun-Sep (hiking); upscale alpine resort, Ladin culture
places.address: 39033 Corvara, Autonomous Province of Bolzano – South Tyrol

places.name: South Tyrol Museum of Archaeology (Ötzi the Iceman)
places.near: Central Old Town, a 5-minute walk from Piazza Walther via Via Museo / near the Talvera river.
places.order: Standard admission with an audioguide; reserve a timed entry slot online to skip queues. Allow 1.5-2 hours.
places.notes: The single most iconic attraction in Bolzano; excellent for families, couples and rainy days.
places.famousFor: Home of Ötzi, the 5,300-year-old naturally mummified 'Iceman' found in the Ötztal Alps in 1991 — one of the world's most important archaeological finds, viewable through a small refrigerated window kept at -6°C and 98% humidity.
places.goodToKnow: Open Tue-Sun 10:00-18:00 (also open Mondays in July & August); closed Jan 1, May 1, Dec 25. Adults ~€13, family (2 adults + children under 16) ~€26, under 6 free. Advance online reservation strongly recommended in high season. Verify 2026 hours/prices on the official site.

places.name: Piazza Walther (Waltherplatz)
places.near: Heart of the Old Town, directly beside the Cathedral; 5-10 min walk from the train station.
places.order: Free to stroll; best at golden hour with the Duomo's tiled roof behind. Host square of the Christmas Market in winter.
places.notes: Ideal starting point for exploring the Old Town on foot.
places.famousFor: Bolzano's grand main square (laid out in 1808), ringed by cafés and pastel façades, with the white-marble monument to medieval minstrel Walther von der Vogelweide at its center. The city's living room and prime people-watching / Instagram spot.
places.goodToKnow: Open public space, always accessible and free. Very lively in summer and during the Christmas Market (late Nov-early Jan). No caveats.

places.name: Bolzano Cathedral (Duomo / Assumption of Our Lady)
places.near: On Piazza Walther in the Old Town.
places.order: Free church entry to admire the interior; visit the Treasury Museum for sacred artifacts. Climb/photograph the ornate spire from the square.
places.notes: Steps from Piazza Walther; pairs naturally with a square visit.
places.famousFor: The region's most important Gothic church (begun 1295, completed early 1500s) with a distinctive green-and-yellow tiled roof, a 65 m filigree bell tower, medieval frescoes, an intricately carved pulpit and the beautiful 'Leitacher Törl' (Wine Door) portal facing Piazza Walther.
places.goodToKnow: Open to visitors outside Mass times (typically Mon-Sat, reduced Sunday access); modest dress required. Treasury Museum has limited opening hours — check locally.

places.name: Runkelstein Castle (Schloss Runkelstein / Castel Roncolo)
places.near: About 2 km / 30-min scenic walk north from the Old Town along the Talvera river path; city bus and a seasonal shuttle bus also serve it.
places.order: Self-guided visit of the West Palace and Summer Palace frescoes; there's a castle tavern on site. Combine with a walk along the Talvera promenade to reach it.
places.notes: A top hidden-gem/Instagrammable castle; the walk there along the river is part of the experience.
places.famousFor: The 'Painted Castle' — home to the largest cycle of secular medieval frescoes in Europe (courtly love, tournaments, and the legend of Tristan and Isolde), dramatically perched on a porphyry crag above the Talvera.
places.goodToKnow: Open Tue-Sun; summer (Mar 15-Nov 2) 10:00-18:00, winter (Nov 3-Jan 7) 10:00-17:00. Closed Mondays and Jan 7-31, 2026 (winter maintenance closure), plus Dec 24-25/31 & Jan 1. Adults ~€10, families ~€20, under 14 free. Verify 2026 dates.

places.name: Messner Mountain Museum Firmian (Castel Firmiano / MMM Firmian)
places.area: Frangarto / Sigmundskron, ~5 km SW of Bolzano
places.near: By car via A22 exit Bolzano Sud then toward Eppan (paid parking ~€4/day, short walk up); bus line 132 from Bolzano station to Schloss Sigmundskron; a summer shuttle runs from Piazza Walther; ~25 min by bike on the cycle path.
places.order: Adult ticket (~€15) for the full castle-and-exhibition circuit; wear sturdy shoes — it's a walk over uneven castle terrain. Combine with the panorama and on-site bistro.
places.notes: Best combined with the panoramic terrace; the walk-through is fairly demanding for small children or limited mobility.
places.famousFor: Reinhold Messner's flagship mountain museum set inside the sprawling medieval Sigmundskron/Firmian Castle, themed on humankind's relationship with the mountains. A labyrinthine route through towers and ramparts delivers spectacular 360° views over the Adige valley and Dolomites.
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal: open ~mid-March to mid-November (2026 season starts Mar 15), 10:00-18:00 (last entry 17:00), CLOSED THURSDAYS. Not fully accessible due to castle stairs/terrain. Verify 2026 dates.

places.name: Renon/Ritten Cable Car (Rittner Seilbahn / Funivia del Renon)
places.near: Valley station on Via Renon, a ~10-min walk from Bolzano train station.
places.order: Round-trip ticket; gondolas depart roughly every 4 minutes. Combine with the historic Renon narrow-gauge railway at the top. Prams and bikes welcome; children under 6 travel free.
places.notes: Gateway to the Ritten plateau, the Earth Pyramids and the Rittnerhorn; great for families and couples.
places.famousFor: A modern panoramic gondola that floats from central Bolzano up to the sunny Ritten (Renon) high plateau at 1,227 m in just ~12 minutes, with sweeping views over the city and Dolomites — one of the easiest 'up into the mountains in minutes' experiences in the Alps.
places.goodToKnow: Runs year-round with high frequency; brief annual maintenance windows possible — check before travel. Buy tickets at the station or via South Tyrol mobility.

places.name: Renon/Ritten Narrow-Gauge Railway (Rittnerbahn)
places.near: Board directly across from the cable-car top station in Soprabolzano/Oberbozen.
places.order: Ride the full line Oberbozen–Klobenstein; combine with the cable car up from Bolzano for a classic half-day loop. Alight at Collalbo to walk to the Longomoso earth pyramids.
places.notes: A genuine heritage-rail gem, delightful with kids and photogenic; pairs with the cable car.
places.famousFor: The last narrow-gauge railway in South Tyrol (opened 1907), running partly with charming historic wooden carriages across the Ritten plateau with postcard Dolomite views the whole way.
places.goodToKnow: Operates year-round on a regular timetable; historic-carriage runs are on select departures. Children under 6 ride free. Check current timetable.

places.name: Earth Pyramids of Ritten/Renon
places.area: Ritten plateau (Katzenbach below Soprabolzano; Longomoso near Collalbo)
places.near: Via the Renon cable car from Bolzano, then the Rittnerbahn railway and a short marked hiking trail.
places.order: Easy family option: take the railway to Collalbo/Klobenstein and walk trail 24 to the Longomoso pyramids (~30 min each way); or from Soprabolzano follow the 'sentiero piramidi' via trail 23 to the Katzenbach viewpoint.
places.notes: Combine cable car + railway + short walk for a superb half-day; the Longomoso approach is the easiest for families.
places.famousFor: Surreal, needle-like clay 'earth pyramids' each topped by a balancing boulder — said to be Europe's tallest and most perfectly formed, carved from glacial moraine over ~25,000 years. Otherworldly and highly Instagrammable.
places.goodToKnow: Free, open-air natural monument. Best in dry weather (clay trails are slippery when wet). Wear proper shoes; viewpoints are fenced. Accessible spring-autumn; winter snow may limit access.

places.name: Corno del Renon / Rittner Horn Cable Car & Summit
places.near: From Bolzano take the Renon cable car + railway to Collalbo, then local bus to the Pemmern lift base (or drive up).
places.order: Ride up to Cima Lago Nero (Schwarzseespitze); families can do the pram-friendly 'Fabulous World of Toni' theme trail at the top, or hike ~1 hour to the summit for the full panorama.
places.notes: Effortless, short and very family/senior-friendly for a big-mountain view; a hidden gem versus the busier Dolomite lifts.
places.famousFor: A cable car from Pemmern up toward the 2,260 m Rittner Horn, delivering one of the widest, most open 360° Dolomite panoramas in the Alps — from the Geisler/Odle to Schlern, Rosengarten, Latemar and beyond.
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal operation (summer hiking season and winter ski season; typically closed in shoulder-season low periods). Dress warmly — it's exposed and high. Verify operating dates.
places.address: Pemmern / Rittner Horn valley station, 39054 Ritten/Renon

places.name: Kohlern / Colle Cable Car (Funivia del Colle)
places.near: Valley station in the Kampenn/Campegno district on the south side of Bolzano (reachable by city bus or a short taxi).
places.order: Round-trip ride, then easy forest walks with viewpoints over Bolzano, the Ritten and Salto plateaus and surrounding peaks. Departs about every 15 minutes.
places.notes: A genuine hidden gem for history buffs and couples seeking calm forest views close to town.
places.famousFor: The world's first cable car built for passenger transport (inaugurated June 1908), climbing 835 m in ~5 minutes to the quiet Colle/Kohlern woodland balcony above Bolzano — a true engineering landmark and a peaceful escape.
places.goodToKnow: Note the daily lunch break in service (approx. 11:00-12:00). Small historic cabins. Check current timetable; quieter than the Renon lift.
places.address: Via Campegno 20 / Kampenner Weg, 39100 Bolzano (valley station)

places.name: San Genesio / Salto (Salten) Plateau & Cable Car
places.near: San Genesio cable car from Bolzano (valley station near the city), ~8 minutes; or by bus/car up to Jenesien.
places.order: Ride the San Genesio cable car up, then gentle walks across the Salto (e.g. toward the Langfenn/Lavena panorama inn); horse-riding is a signature local activity.
places.notes: Underrated nature escape and one of the region's most photogenic plateaus; superb for families and couples.
places.famousFor: Europe's largest/highest larch-tree high plateau — dreamy open meadows dotted with grazing Haflinger horses (whose birthplace is here) and rustic huts, reached by an 8-minute cable car from Bolzano. Golden larches in autumn are spectacular.
places.goodToKnow: Cable car and plateau best May-October; larch colour peaks late October. Easy, family- and senior-friendly walking. Check cable-car timetable.
places.address: San Genesio / Jenesien cable car, 39050 San Genesio Atesino

places.name: Talvera Promenade & Talvera Meadows (Lungotalvera / Talferwiesen)
places.near: Begins at the edge of the Old Town near the Talvera bridges; walkable from Piazza Walther in ~10 min.
places.order: Stroll or rent a bike; walk north toward Runkelstein Castle, or picnic on the meadows. A relaxed sunset walk for couples and families.
places.notes: The city's easiest nature stroll; links several sights together.
places.famousFor: Bolzano's beloved green ribbon along the Talvera river — flat, tree-lined walking/cycling paths past the vast Talvera Meadows, with Maretsch Castle and vineyard slopes as a backdrop and the Dolomites beyond.
places.goodToKnow: Free, open year-round, level and dog-friendly. Connects to Maretsch Castle and (river path) to Runkelstein. No caveats.

places.name: Guncina / Guntschna Promenade
places.near: Cross the Talvera bridge into the Gries district; the promenade starts on the low-traffic streets above Gries.
places.order: Walk the meandering porphyry path uphill from Gries; go on a clear morning for the best views and photos.
places.notes: A hidden gem showcasing Bolzano's famously mild 'Mediterranean-in-the-Alps' microclimate.
places.famousFor: A gently rising, century-old botanical promenade (created 1892) through unexpectedly Mediterranean and exotic vegetation — magnolias, palms, agaves, cork oaks — with panoramic city, Kohlern and Dolomite views. Each plant is labelled.
places.goodToKnow: Free, open year-round; moderate uphill but not strenuous. A sun-trap — pleasant even in cooler months. No caveats.

places.name: Sant'Osvaldo / St. Oswald Promenade
places.near: Starts at the Lungotalvera promenade near the Talvera meadows, north of the Old Town.
places.order: Walk the accessible lower sections from the Talvera meadows for the views; easy and dog-friendly.
places.notes: Confirm the closure has been lifted before planning this specific route. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A ~6 km panoramic promenade climbing from the Talvera meadows toward Sant'Antonio and the Santa Maddalena vineyard hills, with sweeping views over Bolzano and its valleys.
places.goodToKnow: IMPORTANT 2026 CAVEAT: a section between Sant'Antonio and the Ploner/Eberle/Oswaldleiten path junctions has been CLOSED since June 11, 2026 after a rockfall, pending repair works — the lower Talvera/Guncina walks are unaffected alternatives. Check current status before going.

places.name: Maretsch Castle (Schloss Maretsch / Castel Mareccio)
places.near: On the Lungotalvera promenade, a short walk north of Piazza Walther.
places.order: Admire and photograph it from the Talvera promenade with the Dolomites behind; interior access depends on scheduled events/exhibitions.
places.notes: One of Bolzano's most photogenic castles thanks to its vineyard-and-mountain backdrop.
places.famousFor: A picture-perfect 12th-century tower castle with four Renaissance round corner towers — uniquely set among vineyards on the flat valley floor beside the Talvera promenade (famously painted by J.M.W. Turner), now an events/congress venue.
places.goodToKnow: Primarily a conference/events venue — interior not always open to casual visitors; the exterior and vineyard setting are the draw and are freely viewable. Check for public exhibitions.
places.address: Via Claudia de' Medici 12 / Talvera promenade, 39100 Bolzano

places.name: Piazza delle Erbe Market (Obstmarkt / Fruit Market)
places.near: In the Old Town between Via dei Portici and the Talvera side; 3-min walk from Piazza Walther.
places.order: Graze on local speck, alpine cheeses, fresh pretzels, seasonal fruit; roasted chestnuts in autumn. Great for edible souvenirs.
places.notes: The most atmospheric spot in the Old Town; combine with the Arcades right next door.
places.famousFor: Bolzano's daily open-air market, running since 1295 — colourful stalls of South Tyrolean fruit, vegetables, bread, flowers, speck, cheese and spices, anchored by the 1749 Neptune Fountain. A feast for the senses.
places.goodToKnow: Open Mon-Fri 07:00-19:00, Sat 07:00-13:00; CLOSED SUNDAYS. Year-round. Free to browse.

places.name: Via dei Portici (Laubengasse) Arcades
places.near: Runs between Piazza delle Erbe and the Town Hall Square in the Old Town.
places.order: Window-shop and café-hop under the arcades; duck into the narrow deep 'Laubenhäuser' courtyards. A classic Instagram corridor.
places.notes: The commercial and architectural heart of the Old Town; connects directly to the fruit market.
places.famousFor: Bolzano's oldest and most beautiful street — a 300 m run of 12th-century arcaded merchant houses with Gothic arches, oriel windows and wrought-iron signs, lined with elegant boutiques, cafés and traditional shops.
places.goodToKnow: Public street, always accessible; shops typically Mon-Sat with a common midday break at some smaller stores. Busy on Saturdays.

places.name: Dominican & Franciscan Churches (Giotto-School Cloister Frescoes)
places.near: Both in the Old Town — Dominican church near Piazza Domenicani; Franciscan church on Via dei Francescani, both a few minutes' walk from Piazza Walther.
places.order: Free visit to admire the frescoed St. John Chapel (Dominicans) and the Klocker altar and Gothic cloister (Franciscans); a quiet cultural counterpoint to the Cathedral.
places.notes: A genuine art-lover's hidden gem, easily missed by casual visitors.
places.famousFor: Two adjacent Gothic friary churches hiding Bolzano's greatest art treasures: the Dominican church's Chapel of St. John, frescoed by the school of Giotto (c.1330) — the 'jewel' of Gothic painting in South Tyrol — plus the Franciscan church's carved Klocker altar (c.1500) and a peaceful frescoed cloister.
places.goodToKnow: Open outside Mass times; the chapels/cloisters have limited hours and modest-dress rules. Free entry. Verify opening times locally.
places.address: Piazza Domenicani / Dominikanerplatz (Dominican) & Via dei Francescani (Franciscan), 39100 Bolzano

places.name: Museion — Museum of Modern & Contemporary Art
places.near: On the Talvera riverfront in the Centro-Piani district, a short walk from the Old Town across the Talvera.
places.order: Visit the current exhibitions; enjoy the light-filled architecture and the café-bar with river views. Cross the connected footbridge for photos.
places.notes: A sleek modern contrast to Bolzano's medieval core; the free-to-cross bridge and café are pleasant even without a ticket.
places.famousFor: A striking glass-cube contemporary-art museum (opened 2008) spanning the Talvera with its own pedestrian/cycle bridge; a 4,500+ work collection and rotating exhibitions, with the transparent façade used as a light/media surface at night.
places.goodToKnow: Open Tue-Sun 10:00-18:00, Thursdays until ~21:30/22:00; Monday rest day (open Mondays in August). Verify 2026 hours/exhibition schedule.

places.name: Alpe di Siusi / Seiser Alm (Day Trip) — incl. the Sciliar/Schlern
places.near: From Bolzano: bus 170 to Siusi + Siusi–Seiser Alm cable car; or bus 350 to Ortisei + Mont Sëuc cable car. Car access to the plateau is restricted during the day.
places.order: Cable car from Siusi/Seis up to Compaccio/Compatsch, then easy meadow strolls (viewpoints, mountain huts) or the ~3 hr climb toward the Sciliar plateau / Schlernhaus (Rifugio Bolzano) for experienced hikers.
places.notes: The classic family-and-couples Dolomite day trip; doable car-free from Bolzano. The Sciliar summit hike is for fit walkers only.
places.famousFor: Europe's largest high-alpine meadow (56 km²) — rolling green pastures framed by the dramatic Sassolungo and the iconic Sciliar/Schlern with its Santner and Euringer peaks (one of the Dolomites' most photographed skylines). A UNESCO Dolomites showpiece.
places.goodToKnow: Summer cable-car season ~mid/late May to early November (2026: ~21 May-1 Nov). Private-car driving on the plateau is banned 09:00-17:00 — use the lift/bus. Dress for mountain weather.
places.address: Seiser Alm / Alpe di Siusi (Compaccio/Compatsch access), Kastelruth/Castelrotto

places.name: Lago di Carezza (Karersee) — 'Rainbow Lake' Day Trip
places.area: Val d'Ega / Nova Levante, ~26 km SE of Bolzano
places.near: Bus 180 from Bolzano toward Carezza/Karersee (roughly every 30 min, ~50 min ride); or drive the Great Dolomites Road (large paid car park opposite the lake, use the tunnel to cross).
places.order: Walk the easy ~20-min loop trail around the lake for the classic Latemar-reflection shot; go early morning or near sunset for glassy water and fewer crowds.
places.notes: A short, high-impact nature stop — ideal for couples and photographers; combine with the Rosengarten/Latemar area.
places.famousFor: The 'Rainbow Lake' of the Dolomites — an impossibly turquoise-green alpine lake at 1,520 m mirroring the jagged Latemar range; tied to the legend of a sorcerer's shattered rainbow. Among the most photographed spots in South Tyrol.
places.goodToKnow: Swimming and shore access are PROHIBITED to protect the lake; you view it from the fenced loop trail. Free to visit (parking paid). Most vivid colour spring-autumn; can freeze/snow in winter.
places.address: Lago di Carezza / Karersee, 39056 Nova Levante (Welschnofen)

places.name: Catinaccio / Rosengarten (King Laurin's Dolomites) Day Trip
places.area: Val d'Ega / Eggental, SE of Bolzano (Nova Levante / Carezza)
places.near: Bus 180 from Bolzano to Carezza/Nova Levante (~55 min), then the local cable car; or drive the Great Dolomites Road.
places.order: Take the Laurin cable car from Nova Levante/Welschnofen up toward the Kölner Hütte for the alpenglow and photos; fit hikers can do the ~12 km / 800 m circular high-route around the massif from the Kölner Hütte.
places.notes: Best for the sunset 'burning Dolomites' effect; pair with Lago di Carezza in the same valley. Serious hikers only for the full loop.
places.famousFor: The legendary Rosengarten ('Rose Garden') massif that glows fiery crimson at sunset (the 'Enrosadira') — tied to the myth of dwarf-king Laurin's cursed rose garden. A quintessential alpenglow spectacle right on Bolzano's doorstep.
places.goodToKnow: Cable cars and high trails are seasonal (roughly June-October); the circular hike is moderate-to-challenging with rocky/exposed sections and needs good fitness and footwear. Sunset alpenglow is the highlight — plan timing/return transport.

places.name: Bolzano Christmas Market (Mercatino di Natale / Christkindlmarkt) — Seasonal
places.area: Bolzano Old Town (Piazza Walther & around the Cathedral)
places.near: In the heart of the Old Town on and around Piazza Walther; walkable from the train station.
places.order: Local handicrafts and wooden decorations; seasonal food treats like Zelten fruitcake, strudel, roasted chestnuts and Kiachl. Evenings are the most atmospheric.
places.notes: Only relevant for late-Nov to early-Jan visits; confirm 2026 dates before planning. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: Italy's original and most famous South Tyrolean Christmas market (since 1991) — around 80 chalets on Piazza Walther and toward the Cathedral selling handcrafts, wooden toys, candles and Alpine treats amid twinkling lights and a giant tree.
places.goodToKnow: STRICTLY SEASONAL — runs only ~late November to early January (typically last Thursday of Nov through ~Jan 6; closed Dec 25). Exact 2026 dates confirmed later in the year; expect large crowds. Not available outside this window.

places.name: Loacker Café
places.near: On Piazza Walther in central Bolzano, in Palais Campofranco
places.order: Loacker wafer desserts, hot chocolate, cakes and coffee
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Loacker brand cafe overlooking Piazza Walther, serving wafer- and chocolate-based treats
places.goodToKnow: There are two Loacker Cafes in Bolzano (Piazza Walther and the Twenty shopping centre); the saved one is likely the central Piazza Walther location.

places.name: Laboratorio Espresso (Tropical Caffè)
places.area: Don Bosco / Via Milano (west of center)
places.near: About a 15-20 min walk west of Piazza Walther along Via Milano; easy by bus or car (residential area, not the tourist core).
places.order: A single-origin flight tasted three ways (espresso, French press, paper filter); traceable single-origin espresso; V60 pour-over.
places.notes: Best specialty coffee in Bolzano; couples/coffee-lovers over families with strollers.
places.famousFor: Bolzano's original specialty-coffee address; run by coffee-obsessed Luca Pintimalli. Family business since 1981 (on the site of Tropical Caffè, open since 1959), renovated 2020.
places.goodToKnow: The real deal for coffee nerds and a genuine hidden gem - not a scenic old-town terrace but the best cup in town. Beans from artisan micro-roasteries across Italy/Europe are sold to take home. Check opening days (closed Sun; limited weekend hours).

places.name: Konditorei Klaus (Pasticceria Klaus)
places.cuisine: Austro-Hungarian pastry, cakes, South Tyrolean sweets, coffee
places.near: Central, a couple of minutes' walk from Piazza delle Erbe / Via dei Portici in the pedestrian old town.
places.order: Sachertorte; lemon roulade; fruit tarts; apple strudel with a cappuccino.
places.notes: Top konditorei pick; family- and couple-friendly.
places.famousFor: Institution since 1966; widely called the best Sachertorte in Bolzano. Pastry chef Alex's cakes and tortes.
places.goodToKnow: Small terrace on a quiet square plus a cozy interior; great for a mid-morning cake-and-coffee stop. Open Mon-Fri to ~18:30, Sat morning only, closed Sunday.

places.name: Konditorei Peter (Pasticceria Peter)
places.cuisine: Fresh cakes, South Tyrolean apple strudel, coffee
places.near: In the pedestrian historic center with a terrace overlooking a lively market square; walkable from Piazza Walther.
places.order: Südtiroler Apfelstrudel; slice of the day's fresh torte with coffee.
places.notes: Strong strudel spot (Needs verification - confirm exact street address and current hours). (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Charming old-town pastry shop known for freshly made cakes and renowned South Tyrolean apple strudel.
places.goodToKnow: Cozy indoor seating plus a pleasant outdoor terrace for people-watching; good for both couples and families.

places.name: Pasticceria Caffè Lintner
places.cuisine: South Tyrolean strudel & sweets, classic pastries, coffee
places.near: Central, short walk from the old-town core on Via Leonardo da Vinci.
places.order: Apple strudel; ricotta or poppy-seed strudel; Millefoglie; Saint-Honoré.
places.notes: Excellent traditional strudel; family- and couple-friendly.
places.famousFor: Beloved central pastry shop famous for its strudel (apple, plus ricotta and poppy-seed versions) and typical South Tyrolean cakes.
places.goodToKnow: Small and very popular - expect a queue at busy times. One of the few places open on weekends when much of Bolzano closes. Takeaway and custom cakes available.

places.name: Stadt Café Città (Bistrot Città)
places.cuisine: Coffee, cakes, light meals, hot chocolate, gelato
places.near: Directly on Piazza Walther, the central square - impossible to miss.
places.order: Cappuccino or hot chocolate on the terrace; a slice of cake while watching the square.
places.notes: Iconic square-side café; families and couples.
places.famousFor: Classic people-watching café right on Piazza Walther, in the Stadt Hotel Città; famous for its huge international newspaper selection (70+ papers in 10 languages).
places.goodToKnow: Prime square-front terrace under the pink hotel facade - very Instagrammable, especially with the Gothic cathedral behind. Touristy and priced accordingly, but the location and atmosphere are the draw.

places.name: Loacker Café Bolzano - Piazza Walther
places.cuisine: Wafers, chocolate, cakes, coffee, ice cream
places.near: On Piazza Walther in the heart of the old town.
places.order: Wafer-based cakes and desserts; hot chocolate; coffee with a Loacker wafer.
places.notes: Family-friendly brand café; great for kids.
places.famousFor: Café from the iconic South Tyrolean wafer maker Loacker, right on the main square - wafer- and chocolate-themed treats.
places.goodToKnow: Fun, kid-pleasing stop given the famous wafer brand; central square-side terrace. There's also a larger Loacker Café at the Twenty shopping centre (Via G. Galilei 20) with a play-friendly winter garden and sun terrace if you want more space with children.

places.name: Loacker Café Bolzano Twenty
places.area: Twenty shopping centre (Via Galilei, edge of center)
places.cuisine: Wafers, chocolate, cakes, coffee, brunch bites
places.near: 2nd floor of the Twenty shopping centre, Via G. Galilei 20; short bus/car ride or ~15 min walk from the center.
places.order: Wafer sundaes and cakes; cappuccino; build-your-own Loacker gift box from the store.
places.notes: Best Loacker experience for families; open 7 days.
places.famousFor: Flagship Loacker café and brand store on the top floor of the Twenty mall; 200+ wafer & chocolate specialities plus a café.
places.goodToKnow: Spacious with a year-round winter garden and a sun terrace (Feb-Oct) - very family-friendly and open Sundays, when much of the center is shut. Great rainy-day or with-kids option.

places.name: Waag Café (Casa della Pesa / Waaghaus)
places.area: Historic center - near Piazza delle Erbe
places.cuisine: Breakfast, healthy bowls, Italian starters, coffee, aperitivo
places.near: In the pedestrian old town just off Piazza delle Erbe / the Fischbänke lane.
places.order: Breakfast plate; a warm seasonal grain/veg bowl; well-made cappuccino.
places.notes: Cozy, characterful brunch spot.
places.famousFor: Stylish café inside the Casa della Pesa (Waaghaus), one of Bolzano's oldest buildings; blends breakfast/brunch with concerts, art and events.
places.goodToKnow: Design-forward, atmospheric setting in a historic hall - a hidden-gem feel with a cultural program; nice for couples and a hip brunch. Both indoor nooks and outdoor seating.
places.address: Piazza del Grano / Kornplatz area (Waaghaus), 39100 Bolzano

places.name: Exil Lounge Café
places.area: Historic center - Piazza del Grano / Kornplatz
places.cuisine: Coffee, smoothies, light bites, daytime aperitivo
places.near: Central, on Piazza del Grano (Kornplatz) in the pedestrian old town.
places.order: Cappuccino; fresh fruit smoothies; a spritz or aperitivo mid-afternoon.
places.notes: Good coffee-and-people-watching terrace; couples.
places.famousFor: Relaxed contemporary café with a charming terrace tucked under the arcades on Kornplatz - a local favorite for a coffee or early-afternoon break.
places.goodToKnow: Terrace under the arches is atmospheric and photogenic; laid-back rather than trendy. Serves alcohol as part of the aperitivo scene but is a genuine coffee/daytime café, not a bar.

places.name: Vögelino
places.cuisine: Coffee, croissants, focaccia, cake, gelato, aperitivo
places.near: On/just off Piazza Walther in the center.
places.order: Morning cappuccino and croissant; focaccia for a light lunch; afternoon cake or gelato.
places.notes: Convenient central all-day café (Needs verification - confirm exact address and hours).
places.famousFor: Buzzy all-day spot on Piazza Walther - coffee and croissants in the morning through to focaccia, cake and aperitivo; the casual sibling of historic Wirtshaus Vögele.
places.goodToKnow: Great square-side people-watching from morning to evening; flexible for families and couples alike.

places.name: Gelateria Artigianale by Nicolas
places.near: Right on Piazza delle Erbe, Bolzano's lively fruit-and-veg market street in the old town.
places.order: Basil, ginger, or passion-fruit sorbet; yogurt; pistachio and hazelnut for the classics.
places.notes: Top gelato pick; everyone-friendly.
places.famousFor: Widely rated among the very best gelato in Bolzano; clearly homemade, with creative flavors alongside the classics - and remarkable value.
places.goodToKnow: Famously generous scoops at low prices; grab a cone and stroll the market. Very family-friendly and central.

places.name: Gelateria Eccetera
places.near: Central, on Via dei Grappoli, short walk from Piazza Walther.
places.order: Whatever's freshest that day; the fruit sorbets are excellent.
places.notes: Excellent locals' gelateria; family-friendly.
places.famousFor: A Bolzano favorite for 20+ years; a tighter, high-quality flavor list, big portions and low prices - where locals go.
places.goodToKnow: Gelato is gluten-free - handy for coeliacs. Prices roughly EUR 2-5. A locals' hidden gem rather than a tourist showpiece.

places.name: Officina del Gelo Avalon (Gelateria Avalon)
places.cuisine: Artisan gelato with whole, natural ingredients
places.near: In the Bolzano center; walkable from the main square.
places.order: Seasonal fruit flavors and the nut classics; ask what was made that morning.
places.notes: High-quality artisan gelato (Needs verification - confirm exact street address and current hours).
places.famousFor: Small-batch gelato praised for pure, real ingredients and intense fresh flavor - repeatedly called some of the best gelato by visitors.
places.goodToKnow: A quality-first gelateria with a loyal following (~4.1 on Tripadvisor across 170+ reviews). A good hidden-gem alternative to the market-square spots.
places.address: Fiera Bolzano - Messe Bozen, Via Louis Braille - Louis-Braille-Straße, Zona Industriale Bolzano Sud - Industriezone Bozen Süd, Oltrisarco - Oberau, Oltrisarco-Aslago - Oberau-Haslach, Bolzano - Bozen, Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol, 39100, Italia

places.name: Menodiciotto
places.area: Historic center - near Piazza Maria Delago
places.near: Central old town, near Piazza Maria Delago; short walk from the Portici arcades.
places.order: Salted caramel; pistachio; passion fruit; dark chocolate; the strawberry granita is a standout.
places.notes: Friendly, sample-generous gelateria (Needs verification - address is listed the same as Konditorei Klaus by aggregators; confirm exact location).
places.famousFor: Popular gelateria with a wide range of flavors and friendly, English-speaking staff who offer generous samples.
places.goodToKnow: Approachable for non-Italian-speaking visitors thanks to samples and helpful staff - easy with kids.
